name: Estela
dish: Burrata with salsa verde and charred bread; endive with walnuts and anchovy; dry-aged ribeye
notes: Ignacio Mattos. Second-story dining room above a bar that no longer apologizes for itself.

name: Minetta Tavern
dish: Black Label Burger; bone marrow; côte de boeuf for two
notes: Keith McNally. Red-checked tablecloths, caricatures on the walls, and the burger that re-set the bar.

name: Via Carota
dish: Svizzerina; cacio e pepe; insalata verde
notes: Jody Williams and Rita Sodi. The reservation system finally exists; the wait is still a bit mythic.

name: L'Accolade
dish: Natural-wine French bistro: tartare, steak frites, tarte tatin
notes: Newer Bleecker bistro with the candlelit, rough-linen look.

name: I Sodi
dish: Lasagne alle lame di Rita; Negroni on the rocks
notes: Rita Sodi's original Tuscan room, moved north to a bigger space but still with the Negroni program.

name: L'Artusi
dish: Pastas (mushroom sugo; gnocchi); crispy pink-peppercorn octopus
notes: Two levels, white walls, good-wine-list Italian that keeps showing up on the bachelorette itinerary.

name: The Commerce Inn
dish: Shaker-Americana: oysters, pork chop, pilgrim fare reimagined
notes: Jody Williams and Rita Sodi's candlelit ode to early-America cooking. Dark wood and quiet.

name: Corner Bar
dish: Roast chicken for two; the burger that isn't called a burger
notes: Ignacio Mattos at the Nine Orchard. Hotel-lobby glamour without the hotel-lobby feel.

name: Keens Steakhouse
dish: Mutton chop; prime rib; Welsh rarebit
notes: Clay pipes on the ceiling, dark wood, and the mutton chop that's somehow older than anyone at your table.

name: Henry Public
dish: Turkey leg sandwich; Manhattan with the rye you weren't ordering
notes: Tin ceilings, candlelight, a bar so thoroughly of a piece it looks built by costume department.

name: Fairfax
dish: American all-day: eggs, burger, cocktails after
notes: Happy Cooking joint. Corner windows, leather banquettes, all-day crowd.

name: Cecchi's
dish: Continental-American: shrimp cocktail, pork chop, Caesar
notes: Dimly lit red booths and a continental menu pitched to people who'd rather be in 1975.

name: Gramercy Tavern
dish: Tavern-room walk-ins vs. dining-room tasting; roast duck; seasonal vegetables
notes: Danny Meyer. The front tavern is the civilian entry; the back dining room is where you go to propose.

name: KJUN
dish: Korean-Cajun tasting menu
notes: Tiny room, limited seats, tasting only. A chef going after one specific cross-cultural idea.

name: Peking Duck House
dish: Whole Peking duck, carved tableside
notes: The Midtown sibling — order the duck, maybe the scallion pancakes, ignore the rest.

name: Cafe Luxembourg
dish: Steak frites; roast chicken; Lincoln Center crowd at intermission pitch
notes: Keith McNally's UWS bistro. White tile, red banquettes, late-night menu for the post-opera crowd.

name: Sushi 35 West
dish: Omakase at the counter
notes: Midtown sleeper doing serious sushi in a room that looks like it shouldn't.

name: Sushi Yasaka
dish: Lunch omakase; dinner omakase at the counter
notes: Lunch omakase is still one of the best value-to-quality bets north of Lincoln Center.

name: Mitsuru
dish: Sushi counter with a reasonable omakase
notes: Understated Village sushi — the kind of place people keep quiet so they can still get a seat.

name: Waverly Inn
dish: Truffled macaroni and cheese; chicken pot pie; burger
notes: Graydon Carter. Hidden-fireplace Vanity-Fair-in-the-aughts feel; mural, candles, no signage.

name: Wildair
dish: Natural wine + small-plates bar menu
notes: Jeremiah Stone and Fabián von Hauske's more casual Orchard Street room.

name: Contra
dish: Set tasting menu
notes: Flag: Contra closed during the pandemic. Confirm before leaning on the listing — may be the adjacent Wildair project or a new iteration at the address.

name: Bar Oliver
dish: Wine-bar menu: small plates, tinned fish, pasta
notes: Corner-of-nothing Two Bridges spot with the natural-wine crowd on the sidewalk.
